steps.title: Substitute both expressions
steps.content:
Replace $a$ and $b$ in $a-b$ with the expressions from the question. Keep the second expression in parentheses so the subtraction applies to every term.
$$(4p+7q)-(11p-9q+8)$$

steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
The minus sign changes every sign in the second set of parentheses. This turns $-9q$ into $+9q$ and $+8$ into $-8$.
$$4p+7q-11p+9q-8$$

steps.title: Combine matching terms
steps.content:
Combine the $p$-terms and $q$-terms, and keep the constant term.
$$4p-11p=-7p$$
$$7q+9q=16q$$
So the equivalent expression is $-7p+16q-8$. The answer is A.
